name: Lattice-Topped Raspberry Pie
recipeIngredient: 5 cups Whole raspberries (fresh or thawed from frozen)
Pre-made pastry double-crust pie, divided into two balls and chilled
recipeInstructions:
In a saucepan, mix together raspberries, sugar, cornstarch, and salt. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until the mixture comes to a boil and thickens. Remove from heat and let cool for about 1 hour until the mixture reaches room temperature. Divide the dough in half and roll out one portion into a circle that fits into the pie plate, leaving an overhang of dough. Cover with foil and refrigerate.
Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
Pour the cooled filling into the crust. Dot the filling with butter pieces.
Roll the second dough ball into a circle and cut into strips. Arrange the strips in a lattice design over the top of the pie.
Trim the edges of the top and bottom crust, then flute as desired.
Place the pie on a baking sheet and bake until golden brown, about 35-45 minutes. Serve garnished with powdered sugar, chocolate leaves, and whole raspberries if desired.
